issues: The short description incorrectly calls this the 'Centennial Cup Western semifinal' — it should be the 'Centennial Cup Western best-of-seven semifinal', which is accurate, but the bigger error is labeling it a 'Western semifinal' when the OCR and context confirm it is the Western semifinal (4-3 series). Actually that part is fine. The real issue: the short description says 'Centennial Cup Western semifinal 4-3' which is accurate, but omits the notable detail about Jamie Stewart's near-shutout.

The long description omits the notable detail that Red Deer's Pete DeGraaf spoiled goaltender Jamie Stewart's shutout bid at 13:17 of the third period — a colorful and factually verifiable detail present in the OCR.
The headline field is empty — there is no clear standalone headline in the OCR, which is acceptable for a wire brief, so this is not an error per se.
The byline is listed as 'Canadian Press' which matches the OCR attribution '(Canadian Press)' — this is correct.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Dave Tomlinson scored three goals as the Richmond Sockeyes routed the Red Deer Rustlers 9-1 on April 17, 1987, winning the Centennial Cup Western best-of-seven semifinal 4-3.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Dave Tomlinson scored three goals to pace the visiting Richmond Sockeyes to a 9-1 thrashing of the Red Deer Rustlers on April 17, 1987, clinching the Centennial Cup Western best-of-seven semifinal 4-3. Richmond exploded for five goals in the second period, with additional goals from Rob Clarke, Mike Claringbull, Jason Phillips, Jim Gunn, Mike McCormick, and Tony Bobbitt; the Sockeyes outshot the Rustlers 39-34. Red Deer's Pete DeGraaf spoiled goaltender Jamie Stewart's shutout bid at 13:17 of the third period. Richmond advances to face the Humboldt Broncos in the Western best-of-seven final.
review.notes: The draft was largely accurate but omitted the notable Jamie Stewart shutout detail from the OCR. Corrected long description restores this. No headline is present in the OCR — this appears to be a wire brief embedded in the paper. Byline confirmed as Canadian Press.
